comment_text.sn2.10:1.2: Suriya, the sun, is one of the most prominent deities of the Vedas, appearing under many names and aspects as both celestial body and deity.
comment_text.sn2.10:5.1: The Buddha offers an extra verse to the Sun as compared to the Moon.
comment_text.sn2.10:5.3: The mention of “swallow” here confirms this detail of the mythology, which is also implied in the existence of a torture called “Rāhu’s mouth” (MN 13:14.3).
comment_text.sn2.10:5.4: The Buddha is the “kinsman of the sun” as he is a scion of the royal Solar Lineage traced back to the sun himself. Both Sun and Moon attended the Mahāsamayasutta (DN 20:14.3) where, according to the commentary, they became stream-enterers and hence the Buddha’s spiritual “sons”. Symbolically the Buddha, by enabling the Sun’s “release” from the “confined space”, becomes the father of light and wisdom.

root_text.sn2.10:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena sūriyo devaputto rāhunā asurindena gahito hoti.
root_text.sn2.10:1.3: Atha kho sūriyo devaputto bhagavantaṁ anussaramāno tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn2.10:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā sūriyaṁ devaputtaṁ ārabbha rāhuṁ asurindaṁ gāthāhi ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn2.10:6.1: Atha kho rāhu asurindo sūriyaṁ devaputtaṁ muñcitvā taramānarūpo yena vepacitti asurindo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā saṁviggo lomahaṭṭhajāto ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.sn2.10:6.2: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho rāhuṁ asurindaṁ vepacitti asurindo gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:

translation_text.sn2.10:1.2: Now at that time the Sun God had been seized by Rāhu, lord of titans.
translation_text.sn2.10:1.3: Then the Sun God, recollecting the Buddha, at that time spoke this verse:
translation_text.sn2.10:3.1: Then the Buddha addressed Rāhu in verse concerning the Sun God:
translation_text.sn2.10:4.1: “The Sun God has gone for refuge
translation_text.sn2.10:4.2: to the Realized One, the perfected one.
translation_text.sn2.10:4.3: The Buddhas have compassion for the world—
translation_text.sn2.10:5.1: He is a beacon in the darkness,
translation_text.sn2.10:5.2: the blazing sun, circle of awesome might.
translation_text.sn2.10:5.3: Rāhu, do not swallow him as he traverses the sky.
translation_text.sn2.10:5.4: Rāhu, release my offspring, the Sun!”
translation_text.sn2.10:6.1: Then Rāhu, having released the Sun, rushed to see Vepacitti, lord of titans and stood to one side, shocked and awestruck.
translation_text.sn2.10:7.3: and came here looking like you’re in shock:
translation_text.sn2.10:7.4: why do you stand there so scared?”
translation_text.sn2.10:8.1: “My head would have exploded in seven pieces,
translation_text.sn2.10:8.2: I would have found no joy in life,
translation_text.sn2.10:8.3: if, when enchanted by the Buddha’s verse,
translation_text.sn2.10:8.4: I had not released the Sun.”

variant_text.sn2.10:4.3: sūriyaṁ → suriyaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
